---
title: "Emerging status: pre-processing"
author:
- Soria Delva
- Sander Devisscher
- Damiano Oldoni
- Toon Van Daele
- Tim Adriaens

date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    toc_float: true
    number_sections: true
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

This document describes the pre-processing of occurrence data of alien species. The outputs of this pipeline will be used as input for GAM modelling.

# Setup

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, tidy='styler')
```

Load libraries:

```{r load_libraries, message = FALSE}
library(tidyverse) # To do data science
library(tidylog) # To provide feedback on dplyr functions
library(here) # To find files
library(lubridate) # To work with dates
library(rgbif) # To get taxa from publisehd unified checklist
```

# Get data

## Occurrence data

We read the occurrence cube of the PrIUS II taxa generated by script 1_download_custom_cubes.Rmd.
This was generated on 26/01/2026.

NOTE: the species cube is (too) heavy to push to the git. This will give an error when running the script. I needed to download it locally (making connection with zenodo within the script was too slow). So maybe best to download the most recent version of the species cube and put it in the folder data/input before running it.

```{r update cube locally}
fistools::download_gdrive_if_missing(gfileID = "154S4vww2G1f0vH7_jt1cZJXeUEmXlZaL",
                                     destfile = "prius2/input/prius2_species_cube.csv",
                                     update_always = TRUE,
                                     email = Sys.getenv("email"))
```

```{r read_data_cube, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE}
df <- read_csv(
  file =here::here("prius2", "input", "prius2_species_cube.csv"),
  col_types = cols(
    year = col_double(),
    eeacellcode = col_character(),
    specieskey = col_double(),
    occurrences = col_double(),
    mincoordinateuncertaintyinmeters = col_double()
  ),
  na = ""
)%>%
 tidylog::rename(eea_cell_code = eeacellcode,
         taxonKey = specieskey,
         obs = occurrences) %>%
 tidylog::select(-mincoordinateuncertaintyinmeters)%>% #Remove columns we don't need
 tidylog::filter(!is.na(eea_cell_code)) #Remove data not assigned to any cell code

gc()
```


## Grid cells in Belgium

```{r read_regions_info}
gpkg_path <- here::here("prius2", "spatial", "utm1_bel_regions.geojson")
eea_grid_regions <- sf::st_read(gpkg_path)
```


#  Pre-processing

## Add taxonomy 

For better interpretation of the data, we retrieve scientific names (without authorship) of the species whose data we are going to analyze. We also retrieve the class of each alien species to link occurrence data with the related baseline data. This step requires an internet connection and can take long time as we are querying the GBIF API:

```{r get_canonical_names}
#Get taxon keys from the cube
taxon_key <-
  df %>%
  tidylog::distinct(taxonKey) %>%
  pull()

#Extract taxonomic information of these respective taxonkeys
spec_names <- purrr::map_df(
  taxon_key,
  function(k) {
    name_usage(key = k)$data
  }
) %>%
  tidylog::select(
    taxonKey = key,
    canonicalName,
    scientificName,
    kingdomKey, 
    classKey,
    kingdom,
    class
  ) %>%
  tidylog::mutate(canonicalName = ifelse(
    is.na(canonicalName), scientificName, canonicalName
  ))
```

In the rare case multiple taxa share the same scientific name, authorship is added:
  
```{r add_authorship_if_needed}
spec_names <-
  spec_names %>%
  tidylog::group_by(canonicalName) %>%
  tidylog::add_tally() %>%
  tidylog::ungroup() %>%
  tidylog::mutate(canonicalName = if_else(n > 1,
                                          scientificName,
                                          canonicalName)) %>%
  tidylog::select(-c(n, scientificName))
```


## Apply temporal and spatial restraints

We also limit the time series analysis on observations from 2000, conform PrIUS I

```{r define_year_range}
first_year <- 2000
```

Apply temporal restraint to occurrence data:

```{r apply_restraints_time_space_occ_data}
df <-
  df %>%
  tidylog::filter(year >= first_year) #Only keep occurrence data from after 2000
```


## Add information about presence in protected areas

We add whether the grid cell intersects any of the Natura2000 Flemish protected areas and we keep only cells in those

```{r add_natura2000_col}
df <-
  df %>%
  tidylog::left_join(eea_grid_regions,
    by = c("eea_cell_code" = "CELLCODE")
  )%>%
  tidylog::filter(isFlandersN2000)
```


## Create time series

Here we create a dataset with, for each taxonKey, the cells in which this species has been observed as well as the first year the species has been observed anywhere in Belgium.

For each species, define cells with at least one observation:

```{r distinct_cells_taxa}
df_cc <- 
  df %>%
  tidylog::group_by(taxonKey) %>%
  tidylog::distinct(eea_cell_code) %>%
  tidylog::ungroup()
```

For each species, identify the first year with at least one observation:

```{r begin_year_per_species}
df_begin_year <- 
  df %>%
  tidylog::group_by(taxonKey) %>%
  tidylog::summarize(begin_year = min(year))
```

For each species, combine `begin_year` and unique `eea_cell_code` as found above: 

```{r combine_begin_year_cells_taxa}
df_cc <- 
  df_cc %>%
  tidylog::left_join(df_begin_year, by = "taxonKey") %>%
  tidylog::select(taxonKey, begin_year, eea_cell_code)
```


For each cell (`eea_cell_code`) and species (`taxonKey`) we can now create a time series:

```{r make_ts, cache=TRUE}
make_time_series <- function(eea_cell_code, taxonKey, begin_year, last_year ) {
  expand_grid(eea_cell_code = eea_cell_code,
              taxonKey = taxonKey,
              year = seq(from = begin_year, to = last_year))

}

# create timeseries slots
df_ts <- purrr::pmap_dfr(df_cc, 
                         .f = make_time_series, 
                         last_year = year(Sys.Date())
)

## Add data

# Add for each taxonkey in each year and in each cell the number of observations (obs)
df_ts <- 
  df_ts %>%
  tidylog::left_join(
    df %>% tidylog::select(taxonKey,
                           year,
                           eea_cell_code,
                           obs), 
                     by = c("taxonKey", "year", "eea_cell_code"))

#Add for each cell information on whether it belongs to Flanders and to specific N2000 areas
df_ts <-
  df_ts%>%
  tidylog::left_join( 
    eea_grid_regions %>% tidylog::select(CELLCODE,
                           isFlandersN2000,
                           geometry), 
                    by = c("eea_cell_code" = "CELLCODE"))

# Add taxonomic information
df_ts <- 
  df_ts %>%
  tidylog::left_join(spec_names%>% tidylog::select(taxonKey, classKey), 
                     by = "taxonKey")

gc()
```


```{r replace_NAs}

# replace NAs with 0
df_ts <-
  df_ts %>%
  tidylog::replace_na(list( obs = 0))
```

Add column for presence (1) or absence (0)

```{r add_presence_absence}
df_ts <- 
  df_ts %>%
  tidylog::mutate(pa_obs = if_else(obs > 0, 1, 0))
```


Add canonicalName

```{r add_presence_absence}
df_ts <- 
  df_ts %>%
  left_join(spec_names%>% 
                       tidylog::select(canonicalName,taxonKey),
                     by = c("taxonKey"))
```

Arrange order columns:

```{r set_order_columns}
df_ts <-
  df_ts %>%
  tidylog::relocate(taxonKey, 
                    canonicalName,
                    year, 
                    eea_cell_code, 
                    obs, 
                    pa_obs, 
                    classKey,
                    isFlandersN2000,
                    geometry)


```


Define function to create a dataset for the region of interest, containing for each year and taxon, a column with number of observations (obs) and  number of cells occupied (ncells)

```{r lump_geographic_info}
filter_compact_time_series <- function(df, col_to_filter) {
  df %>%
  tidylog::filter(!!sym(col_to_filter) == TRUE) %>%
  tidylog::group_by(taxonKey, canonicalName, year, classKey) %>%
  tidylog::summarise(
    obs = sum(obs),
    ncells = sum(pa_obs)) %>%
  tidylog::ungroup()
}
```

Apply the function to the time series data to create this dataset for the region(s) of interest, in our case the natura2000 areas in Wallonia:

```{r df_ts_compact}
colnames_to_filter <- c("isFlandersN2000")
gc()
df_ts_compact <- purrr::map(
  colnames_to_filter,
  function(x) filter_compact_time_series(df = df_ts, col_to_filter = x)) %>%
  purrr::set_names(colnames_to_filter)

#Remove original df_ts dataset as we don't need it anymore
rm(df_ts)
gc()
```


## Define evaluation period 

We define the time window (in years) we want to assess the emerging status:

```{r define_evaluation_period}
# Last evaluation year
last_year <- lubridate::year(Sys.Date()) - 1

# First evaluation year
first_year <- last_year - 2

# Evaluation years
evaluation_years <- seq(first_year, last_year)
evaluation_years
```

##Remove observations from after the last evaluation year

We remove recent data due to publishing delay. Underestimation of the number of observations would sensibly affect the GAM output.

```{r remove_recent_data}
df_ts_compact <- purrr::map(
  df_ts_compact, 
  function(df) df %>% tidylog::filter(year <= last_year)
)
```

## Remove appearing taxa

Taxa appearing only during the evaluation period (2023-2025) should be removed as no trend can be assessed for them. See [analysis of appearing and reappearing taxa](https://trias-project.github.io/indicators/06_occurrence_indicators_appearing_taxa.html).

```{r define_function_to_remove_appearing_taxa}
# Define function to remove taxa that had never been observed before the evaluation years from the data for modelling
remove_appearing_taxa <- function(df, min_eval_year) {
  df %>%
    # Filter to include only years before min(eval_years)
    tidylog::filter(year < min_eval_year) %>%
    # Group by taxonKey
    tidylog::group_by(taxonKey) %>%
    # Arrange by year within each group to ensure we get the first occurrence
    dplyr::arrange(year, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
    # Filter to keep only the first year where obs > 0 for each taxon
    tidylog::slice(which(obs > 0)[1]) %>%
    # Keep only taxonKey
    tidylog::select(taxonKey) %>%
    # Ungroup to remove grouping
    tidylog::ungroup() %>%
    # Add data from the original data frame
    tidylog::left_join(df, by = "taxonKey")
}

```

```{r remove_appearing_taxa}
# Apply the function to the Natura2000 regions of Flanders
df_ts_compact <- purrr::map(df_ts_compact,
                            remove_appearing_taxa,
                            min_eval_year = min(evaluation_years)
)
```

```{r select_taxonkeys_left_for_modelling}
#Only keep taxonkeys in the taxonomic dataset that are kept for further modelling
spec_names <-
  spec_names %>%
    tidylog::filter(
      taxonKey %in% 
        purrr::pluck(
          purrr::map_dfr(df_ts_compact, function(x) {
            x %>% dplyr::distinct(taxonKey)
          }), "taxonKey")
  )
```


```{r select_taxonkeys_left_for_modelling}
#Only keep taxonkeys in the taxonomic dataset that are kept for further modelling
spec_names <-
  spec_names %>%
    tidylog::filter(
      taxonKey %in% 
        purrr::pluck(
          purrr::map_dfr(df_ts_compact, function(x) {
            x %>% dplyr::distinct(taxonKey)
          }), "taxonKey")
  )
```

# Modelling

In this section we evaluate the emerging status by applying a statistical model called GAM (Generalized Additive Models).
For each evaluation year (see below) the output of both models is one of the following emerging status codes:

- `3`: emerging
- `2`: potentially emerging
- `1`: unclear
- `0`: not emerging


## Generalized additive model (GAM)

We apply GAM to occupancy in the Flemish protected areas using function `trias::apply_gam()` from project package `trias`.

Plots are saved in `./data/output/GAM_outputs` directory:

```{r plots_dir}
dir_name_2000_2025<- here::here("prius2", "output", "GAM_outputs_2000_2025_Occupancy")
if (!dir.exists(dir_name_2000_2025)) dir.create(dir_name_2000_2025)
```

We also define the plot dimensions in pixels:

```{r plots_dimension}
plot_dimensions <- list(width = 2800, height = 1500)
```


We define also a wrap-up function, `apply_gam_for_eval_years` to apply GAM for each evaluation year and taxon:

```{r apply_gam_for_eval_years}
# Define wrap function to apply GAM for every time series (Belgium, its regions
# and protected areas)
apply_gam_for_eval_years <- function(df,
                                     region,
                                     eval_years,
                                     y_var,
                                     indicator,
                                     root_dir,
                                     y_label,
                                     w,
                                     h) {
  # Remove "is" from "isFlandersN2000 subdirectory to be added to the basic directory
  region <- stringr::str_remove(region, "^is")
  dir_name <- here::here(dir_name_2000_2025, region)
  # Check if directory exists before creating
  if (!dir.exists(dir_name)) {
    dir.create(dir_name)
  }
  taxon_keys <- unique(df$taxonKey)
  taxon_names <- unique(df$canonicalName)
  message(paste0("Applying GAM. Variable: ", y_var, "; region: ", region, "."))
  gam_occs <- map2(
    taxon_keys, taxon_names,
    function(t, n) {
      df_key <- df %>%
        dplyr::filter(taxonKey == t)
      class_key <- unique(df_key[["classKey"]])
        # If taxon doesn't belong to any class (classKey is not available), use
        # the default GAM model without covariate (i.e., no baseline_var defined)
        results_gam <- trias::apply_gam(
          df = df_key,
          y_var = y_var,
          taxonKey = "taxonKey",
          eval_years = eval_years,
          type_indicator = indicator,
          taxon_key = t,
          name = n,
          df_title = region,
          dir_name = dir_name,
          y_label = y_label,
          saveplot = TRUE,
          width = w,
          height = h )
      return(results_gam)
    }
  )
  
  names(gam_occs) <- taxon_keys
  gam_occs
}
```



### Occupancy

We apply GAM modelling by using the wrap-up function defined above to occupancy in Belgium, its regions and protected areas. This step can take long.

```{r apply_gam_occupancy_to_all_taxa, message=FALSE, cache=TRUE}
gam_occupancy <- purrr::imap(
  df_ts_compact,
  apply_gam_for_eval_years,
  eval_years = evaluation_years,
  y_var = "ncells",
  indicator = "occupancy",
  root_dir = dir_name_2000_2025,
  y_label = "occupancy (km2)",
  w = plot_dimensions$width,
  h = plot_dimensions$height
)
```


# Save results

## GAM models

Save complete outputs and summaries:

```{r  save_gam_outputs}
# Define function to save GAM model outputs and summaries for Belgium, its
# regions and protected areas
save_gam_output <- function(gam_results,
                            region,
                            indicator) {
  # Save outputs
  readr::write_tsv(
    # Merge the outputs of all taxa as a data.frame for saving
    purrr::map_dfr(gam_results, 
                   function(x) {
                     x$output
                    }),
    na = "",
    file = here::here(
      dir_name_2000_2025,
      paste0("output_GAM_2000_", indicator, "_", region, ".tsv")
    )
  )
  
  # Save summaries
  readr::write_tsv(
    # Merge the summaries of all taxa as a data.frame for saving
    purrr::map_dfr(gam_results, 
                   function(x) {
                     x$em_summary
                    }),
    na = "",
    file = here::here(
      dir_name_2000_2025,
      paste0("summary_GAM_2000_", indicator, "_", region, ".tsv")
    )
  )
}



# Apply function to save decision rules outputs applied to occupancy
purrr::iwalk(gam_occupancy, function(df, region) {
  save_gam_output(df, region, indicator = "occupancy")
})
```

## Plots

We create a zip file with all generated GAM plots:

```{r create-zip-file, results='hide', eval=FALSE}
folder_path <- here::here("prius2", "output","GAM_outputs_2000_2025_Occupancy")

# Get relative paths of PNG files
png_files <- list.files(dir_name_2000_2025,
                        pattern = "\\.png$",
                        recursive = TRUE)

# Change working directory temporarily
old_wd <- getwd()
setwd(folder_path)

# Create zip file
zip(zipfile = "GAM_plots.zip", files = png_files)

# Restore working directory
setwd(old_wd)

#Remove GAM plots after the zip file was created
png_folders<-list.dirs(folder_path,
                       recursive = FALSE)
unlink(png_folders, recursive = TRUE)
```
